title: Specialized Information and Referral * AIDS/HIV Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Specialized Information and Referral * AIDS/HIV Issues 11 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - AIDS/HIV Issues Medicaid Recipients Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO OREGON HIV/STD HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Oregon HIV/STD Hotline has partnered with 211info to provide information and referral for HIV, STDs and general sexual health. Calling the hotline will connect you with a health specialist at 211info who can provide information regarding testing, treatment and prevention of HIV/STDs. Referrals may include low-cost testing, physician referrals, support groups, advocacy and case management services. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Email (800) 777-2437 Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers assistance with orientation and navigation of services, including help with employment and housing applications, short-term rent support, and connections to community resources. 520 NW Davis Street Suite 215 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS and their families Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (503) 223-5907 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT OUR HOUSE NEIGHBORHOOD HOUSING AND CARE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home support like nursing, therapy, and social work. Helps with health checkups, understanding and taking medicines, and getting support for daily and household tasks. Assists with housing needs, connects people with community resources, and offers counseling and social support. Also teaches life and coping skills and checks home safety. 2727 SE Alder Street Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with HIV Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 346-3851 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING BURNS HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 51 W Washington Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 370-2810 401 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING LA GRANDE HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV.
